description: NYC matrimonial e-filing and local procedural notes for MVP counties.

rules.instruction: Matrimonial actions in New York County (Manhattan) are handled through the Matrimonial Part. NYSCEF e-filing is required for attorneys; unrepresented litigants may file in person at 60 Centre Street or by mail when permitted.
After commencement, the court may schedule a preliminary conference. Bring a completed Statement of Net Worth and proposed stipulations if you have them.
Kings County (Brooklyn) matrimonial filings are processed through the Kings County Supreme Court matrimonial part. Verify current NYSCEF requirements and local part rules before filing.
Kings County uses preliminary conferences for case management. Confirm whether your matter is assigned to a judicial hearing officer or judge.
Queens County matrimonial actions are filed with the Queens Supreme Court matrimonial part. Check NYSCEF and local part instructions for indexing and mandatory forms.
Queens matrimonial cases typically receive a preliminary conference order after the summons is filed. Review the order for discovery and net-worth deadlines.
Bronx County matrimonial filings are handled by the Bronx Supreme Court matrimonial part. Confirm e-filing eligibility and any local cover-sheet requirements.
Bronx matrimonial matters may be referred to a preliminary conference early in the case. Bring financial disclosure forms if the conference order requires them.
Richmond County (Staten Island) matrimonial actions are filed with the Richmond Supreme Court matrimonial part. Verify NYSCEF registration and local filing hours.
Richmond matrimonial cases follow the statewide preliminary conference framework. Watch for part-specific scheduling notices after service is complete.
